item.title: Trump defends ICE efforts during traffic stops after fatal shootings in Maine and Texas
item.description: Every ICE arrest team will have at least one officer wearing a body camera, the Department of Homeland Security announced following two fatal shootings by ICE agents who were not wearing bodycams. President Trump defended ICE's operations on social media amid the policy changes. Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more details.

item.title: Subaru recalls more than 541,000 vehicles over labeling error
item.description: Erroneous labels on some Subaru Crosstrek, Forester and Ascent cars could increase the risk of a crash, NHTSA said.

item.title: ICE halts most vehicle stops and increases bodycam use after fatal shootings in Texas and Maine
item.description: Every ICE arrest team will have at least one officer wearing a body camera, the Department of Homeland Security announced following two fatal shootings by ICE agents who were not wearing bodycams. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more details.

item.title: DoD watchdog finds 155mm artillery plant built 2 years ago has produced nothing
item.description: An ammunition plant in Mesquite, Texas, has not produced any metal projectile parts after the Army spent $469 million to establish the facility.

item.title: U.S. Mint to make $1 gold coin with Trump's face on it
item.description: It's the latest effort by President Trump to make his mark on Washington, D.C.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: Can debt collectors come after your inheritance?
item.description: Worried that creditors will seize your inherited assets over unpaid debt? Here's the likelihood of that happening.

item.title: Spain upsets France to head to World Cup final – but who will join them?
item.description: Spain is on its way to the World Cup final after upsetting France, the favorite to win the title. On Wednesday, defending champion Argentina will take on England, with the winner advancing to Sunday's final. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Wildfires expose millions in Midwest, Northeast to dangerous smoke
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: The measure also allows states to opt out if they take action before the federal law is enacted.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Watch Live: Jay Clayton, Trump's DNI nominee, testifies at confirmation hearing
item.description: Clayton, currently the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, is expected to succeed Bill Pulte, Trump's controversial acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Lawmaker calls ICE a "lawless, rogue organization" as tensions rise over recent fatal shootings
item.description: Illinois Democratic Rep. Jesús "Chuy" García joined CBS News with his reaction to two recent fatal shootings involving ICE agents in Texas and Maine.

item.title: U.S. announces new strikes on Iran as war intensifies
item.description: The U.S. announces new strikes on Iran just hours after a 4th consecutive nighttime barrage, as the war ramps back up over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: A look at the history behind England and Argentina's rivalry ahead of World Cup match
item.description: England and Argentina are set to face off, with the winner taking on Spain in the World Cup final. The two countries have a long history in both politics and in the sport. Leigh Kiniry reports.

item.title: World Cup highlights and what to expect in England vs. Argentina match
item.description: Poppy Miller, lead host for CBS Sports Golazo, previews the World Cup match between England and Argentina and breaks down Spain's win over France.

item.title: "Miracle on the Hudson" pilot, Capt. "Sully," shares Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Capt. Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger, who famously landed a passenger jet on the Hudson River in 2009, said he has been diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease.

item.title: Battle against data centers grows nationwide amid concerns over electricity, water usage
item.description: There's growing backlash nationwide against AI data centers and now New York is hitting pause. Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order to delay the building boom. Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Officer who rescued woman on Brooklyn Bridge was in first week on elite unit
item.description: Officer Christian Yepes helped to save a woman's life on the Brooklyn Bridge last week. It was only his third day on the job with the NYPD Emergency Service Unit. Anna Schecter reports.

item.title: How to protect yourself from the diarrhea-causing parasite as cyclosporiasis cases rise
item.description: Federal and state health officials are looking for the source of a cyclosporiasis outbreak as cases rise in the U.S. Carter Evans has more information on the spread of the cyclospora parasite.

item.title: Capt. "Sully" Sullenberger, who landed passenger jet on the Hudson, diagnosed with Alzheimer's
item.description: Capt. Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger, who saved 155 lives by safely landing a plane in the Hudson River in 2009, revealed he's been diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Texas braces as deluge of rain brings dangerous flash flooding conditions
item.description: Some parts of Texas are under a state of emergency as torrential rain heightens the risk of flash flooding. This comes a little over a year after flood waters brought tragedy to the Texas Hill Country, killing over a hundred people. Jason Allen reports.

item.title: House passes bill that could make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: Lawmakers in the House passed a bill on Tuesday with bipartisan support that would make daylight saving time permanent and end the twice-a-year time change. It's now up to the Senate to take up the legislation. Caitlin Huey-Burns has more.

item.title: Watch Live: Blanche testifies at confirmation hearing for attorney general
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche appearing before the Senate Judiciary Committee for his confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department in a permanent capacity.

item.title: Introducing Norah O'Donnell's new health podcast
item.description: "Healthful with Norah O'Donnell" launches July 22.

item.title: Heavy rain hits Texas and intense heat impacts parts of U.S.
item.description: More than a foot of rain fell in parts of Texas in just 24 hours, leading to dozens of rescues. Meanwhile, heat warnings stretch from Southern California to the Northeast while heavy rain slams Texas. Jason Allen and Rob Marciano have the latest.

item.title: England-Argentina World Cup semifinal reignites painful historic rivalry
item.description: The World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina is a grudge match that will reopen decades-old wounds for both countries.

item.title: Alzheimer's blood test may show who's likely to develop it in 5 to 10 years
item.description: A blood test may predict if apparently healthy older adults are likely to develop Alzheimer's symptoms in the next five or 10 years, researchers say.

item.title: 26 Meta workers sue over alleged AI-aided layoffs of employees on leave
item.description: A group of 26 Meta employees has sued the company, claiming it used AI to choose people for layoffs, disproportionately targeting those on medical, parental or family leave.

item.title: Ukraine's prime minister resigns; Zelenskyy shifts several government roles
item.description: Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko has stepped down as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced fresh changes to Ukraine's government.
